translated: Like, "How much dancing is there?"
We were all having conversations like that,
but I happened to run into Yabu right after she finished learning the choreo for Mei-chan's song.
That's when she whispered to me, "It turned out to be flamenco..."
And I was like, "Wait, hold on. What do you mean?"
I was just so confused. Like, "Flamenco?"
Like Yabu was saying earlier,
I imagined her standing tall at a stand mic,
with a burning passion, but then I heard it was flamenco.
You don't really get many chances to see flamenco, you know?
Liella probably wouldn't do it either.
No, we wouldn't.
All of us doing flamenco together...
That's unlikely.
It must be hard when you don't know what the "right" way is.
It looks difficult just watching, so doing it yourself must have been a huge challenge.
It's incredibly difficult.
But I saw how much she practiced, so...
Yeah, her moves were so sharp.
Right? She looked so cool.
That's why...
Like Miku-san said, the part where you thrust your fist forward felt so direct and very Mei-chan-like.
I love it.
Yeah, I totally get it.
It didn't feel like just "choreography" anymore.
Yeah.
Mm-hmm.
It felt like you were thrusting your fist out of pure emotion.
I found myself getting really emotional during that part, too.
Yeah.
That part happens at the end of the first verse,
the end of the second verse,
and then after the final chorus, the song ends with it too.
The movement is exactly the same every time, but...
I could feel that the emotions behind it were different in the first, second, and final parts.
Depending on the performance, what I'm feeling in that moment changes,
so I put all of my feelings into that fist.
Yeah, I see.
That's great.
Thank you.
I really love the sound of the glass shattering.
Oh, yeah.
That part is so cool.
Isn't it?
It stands out even more during a live performance.
Ah, definitely.
It really resonated, and the flames in the background...
matched Mei-chan perfectly and looked so cool.
Yeah.
And right after that, we had "Fantastic," so...
Right, that's true.
Yeah.
We were preparing, so I was watching Mei-chan's performance from the wings.
Feeling her heat, the two of us were like, "Alright, let's do this!"
"Let's keep this momentum going!"
It was really, really great.
It was so cool.
Thank you so much.
Can I say something too? Kanon performs right before Mei-chan,
and since there's a bit of time before Kanon's next appearance,
I was able to see Mei-chan right before she went on to sing.
I saw her from backstage.
And of course, it was the same before that,
but I'd also run into Yabu right after her performance.
She always had this look of having given it her all with such passion,
but at the same time, she never seemed fully satisfied.
She'd have this expression like, "What should I do better next time?"
That's how Yabu always looked.
It's like... how do I say it?
She had her own sense of accomplishment from giving her all,
but she always had this look on her face like, "I've still got a long way to go."
Backstage.
That just felt so much like Mei-chan to me,
that I almost started crying while drinking my hot water alone.
I really did.
That's so Mei-chan.
From our perspective, we're all thinking, "You were wonderful!"
Everyone feels that way,
but she's just... how do I put it?
Her own... what's the word?
